title: 10 best herbal capsules for ulcerative colitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root causes of the disease. Curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation in the colon, alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea, and even induce remission in some cases. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage, promoting healing and reducing scarring in the digestive tract.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory effects: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, which is a hallmark of UC.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals and prevent damage to the gut lining, reducing symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and bloody stools associated with UC.

Immunomodulatory effects: Curcumin has been shown to modulate the immune response in the gut, reducing inflammation and promoting a balance between pro-inflammatory and anti-inflammatory cytokines, which can help mitigate UC symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, thereby alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another polyphenolic compound in Curcuma longa, inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are molecules that promote inflammation, and helps to reduce the severity of ulcerative colitis symptoms.

Beta-sitosterol: Beta-sitosterol, a phytosterol, has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the colon, thereby providing relief from ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used due to their high curcumin content, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Roots: Utilized for their rich source of curcuminoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
Leaves: Employed for their volatile oil content, including beta-pinene and limonene, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Dry curcuma longa roots in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind 1 gram of dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground curcuma longa powder with 1 gram of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of water.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the curcuma longa mixture using a small spoon.
Store the herbal curcuma longa capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the colon, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The mucilage present in slippery elm acts as a natural barrier, shielding the gut lining from irritants and toxins that can exacerbate symptoms of UC. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation, allowing for improved digestion and nutrient absorption. This natural remedy provides relief from ulcerative colitis symptoms, promoting overall gut health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slipping elm's mucilages form a protective barrier on the gut lining, reducing inflammation and irritation by coating and soothing the mucous membranes.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Slippery elm contains anti-inflammatory compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the colon, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Demulcent properties: The demulcent properties of slippery elm create a soothing, protective barrier on the gut lining, allowing for a more comfortable passage of food and stool while reducing irritation and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with ulcerative colitis.

Flavonoids: Specifically, quercetin, a flavonoid found in Ulmus rubra, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, a common issue in ulcerative colitis.

Phenolic acids: These compounds, including ellagic acid and gallic acid, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gut lining and reduce inflammation in ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Barks: Barks are used to make capsules for ulcerative colitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make capsules for ulcerative colitis due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: Roots are used to make capsules for ulcerative colitis due to their potential to reduce inflammation and improve gut health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried ulmus rubra bark from mature trees at the end of summer season.
Dry the harvested material at 40c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture completely.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 1g of the ground powder with 20g of gelatin and 20g of glycerin in a clean bowl.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules and store them in an airtight container immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain mucilages, which are natural soothers that coat and protect the inflamed colon lining. These capsules reduce inflammation and irritation by forming a protective barrier against irritants and toxins, allowing the gut to heal naturally. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloating associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with Ulcerative Colitis.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and saponins, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of Ulcerative Colitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Soothing demulcents: The demulcent properties of marshmallow root create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, coating and soothing them to reduce irritation, discomfort, and inflammation associated with Ulcerative Colitis.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages in Althaea officinalis have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the inflamed mucous membranes in the colon, reducing symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid in Althaea officinalis, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, which are key contributors to ulcerative colitis.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid): Caffeic acid, a phenolic acid in Althaea officinalis, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and protect against cellular damage in the colon, thereby alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The root is the most commonly used part of Althaea officinalis for making capsules for ulcerative colitis due to its high mucilage content.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and protect the digestive tract in ulcerative colitis.
Stems: The stems of Althaea officinalis are used for their mucilaginous properties, which help to coat and protect the gastrointestinal tract in ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of althaea officinalis roots in the morning after the dew has dried.
Clean the roots by gently scrubbing them with a soft-bristled brush under cold running water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a low-temperature oven at 60c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 200mg of the powdered root material each.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they promote digestive health by soothing and repairing the lining of the colon, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. The anti-inflammatory compounds in aloe vera, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, have been shown to decrease prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause pain and inflammation in the gut. Additionally, aloe capsules can help restore the balance of beneficial gut bacteria, improving digestion and reducing symptoms associated with ulcerative colitis, including diarrhea, abdominal pain, and bloody stools.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and barbaloin, which help reduce inflammation in the colon, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Soothing and protective properties: Aloe vera's gel has soothing and protective properties that help heal and protect the mucous membranes lining the colon, reducing damage caused by chronic inflammation and promoting tissue repair.

Antioxidant activity: Aloe vera contains antioxidants such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, further contributing to its anti-inflammatory and protective effects.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloein's anti-inflammatory and laxative properties can help to reduce inflammation and promote bowel movements, potentially alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the colon, potentially leading to improved symptoms in individuals with ulcerative colitis.

Aloinic acid (also known as C-glucosyl-8-hydroxyquinoline): Aloinic acid has been found to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria in the gut, which may help to reduce the severity of ulcerative colitis by mitigating the effects of gut dysbiosis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloe-emodin, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the gut lining in ulcerative colitis.
Gel (derived from Leaves): Contains a high concentration of mucilages, which helps to protect and heal the gut lining, reducing inflammation and symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Aloe juice (derived from Leaves): Has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis, such as diarrhea and abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 aloe vera leaves with thick fleshy parts to ensure sufficient gel extraction.
Cut off the aloe vera leaves' spines with a sharp knife or kitchen shears.
Peel the leaves' outer skin to expose the transparent gel inside the leaf's fleshy part.
Extract the aloe vera gel using a blender or food processor for 2-3 minutes.
Fill gel-filled capsules using a capsule machine or spoon with a 500mg dosage per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient reduces inflammation in the colon, which is a primary symptom of ulcerative colitis. The anti-inflammatory effects also help to heal damaged mucous membranes and reduce bowel symptoms such as diarrhea and abdominal pain. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to stimulate the production of mucus in the digestive tract, protecting it from further irritation and damage. As a result, licorice capsules can provide relief for individuals with ulcerative colitis, improving their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, thus alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Flavonoids: Possess antimicrobial and antispasmodic activities, which can help combat harmful gut bacteria and relax smooth muscle contractions in the digestive tract, thereby reducing symptoms such as diarrhea and abdominal pain.

Isoliquiritigenin: Has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the colon, potentially leading to improved remission rates and reduced symptom severity.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Glycosides (Glycyrrhizin and Isoglycyrrhizin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Flavonoids (Isoliquiritigenin and Liquiritigenin): These flavonoids possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help protect the gut mucosa from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in ulcerative colitis.

Terpenoids (Liquiritigenin and Glycyrrhetol): These terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that help reduce inflammation, prevent the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, and promote a healthy gut microbiome in ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used for their flavonoids and saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Barks: They are used due to their triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer when the plant is in full maturity stage.
Clean and dry the harvested roots in a warm air circulation oven at 50°c for 3 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix the powdered roots with a binding agent like gelatin or starch in a 1:5 ratio to create a uniform mixture.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules in size 0 using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turpeth capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes the gut lining, reducing inflammation and irritation. The herbs in the capsule, such as Turpeth root, also help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for easier passage of stool and alleviating symptoms like abdominal pain and cramping. Additionally, Turpeth capsules promote healing by increasing blood flow to the colon, which helps to repair damaged tissue and promote a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_properties: Purgative property: Turpeth capsules stimulate bowel movements, helping to clear out toxins and relieve constipation, which is a common symptom of Ulcerative Colitis.

Anti-inflammatory property: The herb Turpeth has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and rectal bleeding associated with Ulcerative Colitis.

Antioxidant property: Turpeth capsules contain antioxidants that help protect the mucous membrane lining of the colon from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healing in cases of Ulcerative Colitis.
remedy_constituents: Turpethine: Turpethine, an alkaloid found in Operculina turpethum, is believed to help with ulcerative colitis by reducing inflammation and promoting bowel movements due to its laxative properties.
Steroids: Steroids, such as sitosterol and stigmasterol, found in Operculina turpethum, may help with ulcerative colitis by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside found in Operculina turpethum, may help with ulcerative colitis by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune system, and promoting the healing of the gut lining.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Seeds: They are used because they contain turpethin, a compound that has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Dry the chopped roots of operculina turpethum in a clean and dry environment for 2 days to prevent moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered roots with a binding agent like gum acacia in a 1:1 ratio by weight.
Fill a capsule mold with the powdered root mixture using a small spoon for 5 minutes.
Store the herbal capsules in a cool and dry place away from direct sunlight for 2 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called gingerol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the colon. This can help alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloody stools that are characteristic of the condition. Additionally, ginger's ability to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility can also aid in reducing symptoms and promoting healing in the affected areas.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ginger capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger has potent antioxidant properties that help protect the colon from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate ulcerative colitis symptoms.

Prostaglandin inhibition: The bioactive compounds in ginger capsules have been shown to inhibit prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause inflammation and pain in the gut, contributing to the development of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, specifically 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the colon and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly 6-shogaol, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect the gut lining from damage and reduce oxidative stress associated with ulcerative colitis.

Zingerone: Zingerone, a compound derived from ginger, has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help mitigate the symptoms of ulcerative colitis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are used for their bioactive compounds like gingerols, which help in reducing inflammation and pain associated with ulcerative colitis.
Stems: Stems of the plant are sometimes used, but their exact mechanisms in treating ulcerative colitis are less documented than rhizomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 dried zingiber officinale roots and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Grind 5 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered root with 250 milligrams of a natural filler agent such as rice flour.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the root-filler mixture in 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the colon and alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and cramping. The capsules also contain mucilages that soothe and protect the lining of the colon, preventing further damage from occurring. Additionally, fenugreek has been shown to increase the production of mucus, which helps to heal and repair damaged tissue in the colon, promoting a faster recovery for individuals with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fenugreek capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea associated with ulcerative colitis.

Gastroprotective: The mucilages present in fenugreek capsules can form a protective barrier on the mucosal lining of the colon, preventing further damage and irritation caused by inflammatory processes and protecting against gastrointestinal ulcers.

Antioxidant: Fenugreek capsules contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and tissue damage in ulcerative colitis, thus reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids found in Trigonella foenum graecum may exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which could help soothe the gut lining and reduce inflammation associated with ulcerative colitis.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly ferulic acid, may possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which could help protect the gut from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Trigonella foenum-graecum are used to make capsules for ulcerative colitis due to their high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Trigonella foenum-graecum are used to make capsules for ulcerative colitis due to their high levels of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

Seeds: The seeds of Trigonella foenum-graecum are also used to make capsules for ulcerative colitis due to their high content of isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried trigonella foenum graecum leaves and flowers with a sharp tool under good lighting.
Grind the 50 grams of dried herbal material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 2 grams of silica gel with the fine powder and stir well to facilitate capsule formation for 5 minutes.
Use an encapsulation machine to fill 200 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture in 30 minutes.
Label and store the 200 herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms in the colon. The menthol in peppermint relaxes the muscles in the gut, allowing for a more regular bowel movement and reducing symptoms such as diarrhea and abdominal pain. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation in the intestine, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Herbal peppermint capsules contain menthol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and bleeding.

Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint's essential oil, particularly its menthol content, helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can exacerbate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Prokinetic effects: Peppermint has been found to increase the movement of food through the digestive system, which can help alleviate symptoms such as constipation and bloating associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon associated with ulcerative colitis.
Menthol: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help reduce inflammation and prevent infections in the colon, thereby alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Luteolin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, thereby alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the colon.
Roots: Employed for their antioxidant properties, which can help protect the colon from oxidative stress and damage.
Stems: Utilized for their antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial overgrowth in the gut often associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves from a pesticide-free area.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 30 grams of the powdered leaves with 60 grams of vegetable cellulose in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal the capsules using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and heal the digestive tract. The plant's bitter compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the colon, which can help alleviate symptoms of UC, including abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloating. Additionally, dandelion root has been found to have antimicrobial properties, which may help combat harmful bacteria that contribute to the development of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the colon and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's antioxidants can help protect the colon mucosa from oxidative damage caused by chronic inflammation, reducing the risk of tissue damage and promoting healing.

Gut-friendly compounds: Dandelion contains prebiotic fibers and inulin that can help stimulate the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, improving digestive health and potentially reducing symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Luteolin: This flavonoid phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Taraxasterol acetate: This triterpenoid saponin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the gut, providing relief from ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Roots: Utilized for their prebiotic properties, which can help regulate gut bacteria and promote a healthy gut microbiome.
Seeds: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe and protect the colon lining.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves on a sunny morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean and dry the harvested taraxacum officinale thoroughly to prevent contamination and moisture content.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 10 minutes.
Mix 1g of the taraxacum officinale powder with 2g of gelatin and 1g of water to create a uniform blend at 5 minutes.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with the taraxacum officinale blend and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a fatty acid that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. GLA works to reduce inflammation in the gut, which is a primary symptom of ulcerative colitis. Additionally, borage capsules have been shown to increase the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that help to heal and protect the mucous membranes lining the colon. This can lead to a reduction in symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and bloody stools associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Borage oil's gamma-linolenic acid (GLA) helps reduce inflammation in the gut, which can alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in borage capsules may help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to UC symptoms like bloating and fatigue.

Prostaglandin regulation: GLA from borage oil has been shown to regulate prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that play a role in inflammation and gut health, potentially reducing UC symptoms such as bloody stools.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of borage contain a high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
Seeds: Borage seeds are rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator on low temperature for 24 hours or air drying method.
Combine 20 grams of dried borago officinalis with 40 grams of potato starch in a bowl and mix well.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture, aiming to achieve approximately 500 mg per capsule.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months or as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rhubarb capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the colon. The capsules also stimulate bowel movements and help to soften stool, which can alleviate symptoms of constipation, a common issue for people with ulcerative colitis. Additionally, rhubarb has been traditionally used to treat gastrointestinal issues, including inflammatory bowel disease, making it a natural and effective treatment option for managing ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Astringent property: Rhubarb's astringent property helps to reduce inflammation and shrink swollen tissues in the colon, which can provide relief from diarrhea, abdominal pain, and other symptoms associated with ulcerative colitis.

Anti-inflammatory property: Rhubarb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as anthraquinones and tannins, may help to reduce inflammation in the colon lining, which is a hallmark of ulcerative colitis, thereby reducing symptoms like rectal bleeding, abdominal pain, and bowel frequency.

Laxative property: Rhubarb's laxative effects can help to regulate bowel movements, alleviate constipation, and promote the elimination of toxins and inflammatory mediators from the body, which can help to reduce symptoms of ulcerative colitis such as bloating, abdominal distension, and rectal bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the rhubarb plant have been used for centuries in traditional Chinese medicine to treat digestive issues, including ulcers and inflammation, due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhubarb rhyzomes, the underground stems that connect the roots to the leaves, contain anthraquinone glycosides, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Measure 200g of dried rhizoma rheum officinale powder into a clean glass container.
Combine 1000ml of boiling water with 20g of dried astragalus membranaceus root powder and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the astragalus mixture through a cheesecloth into a separate container discarding the solids and add to the rhizoma rheum officinale powder.
Mix well and let it sit at room temperature for 2 hours before filling capsules with 500mg of the herbal blend per capsule.
Fill empty #0 capsules with the herbal mixture using an automatic capsule filler or scoop out the correct amount manually.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain berberine, a bioactive compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut. Berberine has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis, such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and rectal bleeding. Additionally, barberry capsules have antimicrobial properties, which can help to eliminate harmful bacteria from the gut, promoting a healthy balance of gut flora and reducing inflammation. By targeting both inflammation and gut imbalance, berberine-rich barberry capsules may provide relief for individuals suffering from ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Berberine: A key alkaloid found in barberry, berberine has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and infection in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Vasodilatory effects: Barberry contains compounds that cause blood vessels to dilate, which may help increase blood flow to the affected areas of the colon, promoting healing and reducing symptoms such as diarrhea and abdominal pain.
Antioxidant activity: Barberry's antioxidant properties can help protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the barberry plant have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and treating infections associated with ulcerative colitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the barberry plant contain berberine, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce symptoms of ulcerative colitis by reducing inflammation and improving gut health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried roots of berberis vulgaris from a trusted source and store them in an airtight container.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the ground berberis vulagris powder with 200mg of dried psyllium husk and 100mg of marshmallow root powder in a bowl.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.
Take three capsules twice daily, one hour before meals, for 3 months to help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The capsules contain saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, the antioxidants present in maidenhair tree extracts help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the mucous membrane lining the colon, reducing symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and rectal bleeding associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, alleviating symptoms of Ulcerative Colitis.

Saponins: Saponins have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, which may help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the gut, improving symptoms of Ulcerative Colitis.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gut lining from damage and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms of Ulcerative Colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain bioactive compounds like saponins and triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the gut, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the maidenhair tree are rich in polysaccharides and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to inhibit inflammation and oxidative stress, making them effective for treating chronic gastrointestinal disorders like ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source, ensuring freshness and potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 24 hours or air-drying method for 7 days.
Crush the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, requiring approximately 1:4 ratio of leaves to powder.
Mix 400mg of the powdered ginkgo biloba with 100mg of cornstarch and 50mg of magnesium stearate for optimal blending, taking 10 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the blended mixture using a capsule filler machine or manual filling process, requiring 500mg per capsule and yielding approximately 200 capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is a major contributor to symptoms of ulcerative colitis. Additionally, elder capsules may also stimulate the production of gut-friendly bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and reducing symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and fatigue associated with ulcerative colitis. By targeting both inflammation and gut health, elder capsules can help alleviate symptoms and improve quality of life for individuals suffering from this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Elderberry's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gut, which can exacerbate ulcerative colitis symptoms.

Astringent and antimicrobial properties: The astringent properties of elderberry help soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the colon, reducing irritation and discomfort, while its antimicrobial properties may help eliminate harmful bacteria that contribute to inflammation.
Vasoprotective effects: Elderberry's vasoprotective compounds help maintain the integrity of blood vessels in the gut, reducing the risk of bleeding and improving overall digestive health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with ulcerative colitis.

Barks: Elder bark contains flavonoids and terpenoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis such as diarrhea and abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and crush them into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the crushed sambucus nigra powder with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar and let it steep for two hours at room temperature.
Filter the liquid mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the clear liquid extract.
Evaporate the extract to dryness using a rotary evaporator or by placing the container in a warm water bath at 60°c for three hours.

Fill empty gelatin capsules with the resulting sambucus nigra powder, dosing each capsule with 250 milligrams of the herb and storing them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, which is a common symptom of ulcerative colitis. Additionally, lemon balm has been found to increase the production of mucus in the digestive tract, which can help to soothe and protect the lining of the colon. This can lead to improved digestion, reduced symptoms, and better overall quality of life for individuals with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to the development and progression of ulcerative colitis.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and terpenes present in lemon balm have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, reducing swelling and irritation in the colon and alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Gastroprotective properties: Lemon balm's ability to stimulate gastric mucus secretion and enhance mucin production can help protect the digestive lining from damage caused by inflammation, acid reflux, and other factors associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a higher concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Flowers: Lemon balm flowers are rich in flavonoids, particularly apigenin, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in soothing and calming digestive issues associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted source in amounts sufficient for the desired batch size.
Crush the dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered melissa officinalis with 500mg of gelatin capsules in a small bowl to achieve a uniform blend.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, making sure each capsule contains the same amount of the blended herbs.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain the herb Achillea millefolium, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and protect the gut lining. The capsules may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and bloody stools. Additionally, yarrow's ability to stimulate digestion and absorption can improve nutrient uptake and overall gut health, further aiding in the management of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow capsules contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the colon, a primary symptom of ulcerative colitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in yarrow capsules help neutralize free radicals that can damage gut tissue and contribute to chronic inflammation, making it an effective adjunctive treatment for ulcerative colitis.

Gastroprotective effects: Yarrow's gastoprotective compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, have been shown to reduce the production of acid in the stomach, protect against ulcers, and promote healing, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with ulcerative colitis.
Flowers: Containing sesquiterpene lactones and chamazulene, these compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe the gut lining and reduce symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves and stems of achillea millefolium plants in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or machine drying for at least 24 hours.
Grind one part dried achillea millefolium into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one teaspoon of ground achillea millefolium with three capsules of gelatin in a small bowl to create the capsule filling.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for ulcerative colitis are a natural remedy that utilizes plant-based extracts to alleviate symptoms and manage the disease. These capsules contain herbs such as turmeric, ginger, and slippery elm, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the colon and alleviate abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloody stools associated with ulcerative colitis. For example, curcumin-rich turmeric capsules have been shown to significantly improve symptoms and quality of life in patients with active ulcerative colitis. By providing relief from symptoms, herbal capsules can improve overall well-being and enable individuals to live more normal lives despite their condition.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ulcerative colitis that are treated with herbal capsules are Crohn's disease, irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), and diverticulitis. Herbal supplements like Turmeric/Curcumin, Ginger, Slippery Elm, Marshmallow Root, and Aloe Vera have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and constipation. These herbal capsules can also promote healing and prevent further damage to the gut lining.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with ulcerative colitis is Turmeric/Curcumin, Boswellia, and Slippery Elm. Turmeric/Curcumin contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory that reduces inflammation and promotes healing. Boswellia, also known as frankincense, helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. Slippery Elm soothes the gut lining, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. This combination works synergistically to reduce symptoms, promote healing, and improve quality of life for those with ulcerative colitis. Consult a healthcare professional before adding any supplements to your regimen.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Planta medica, medicinal plants have been found to be an effective alternative treatment for ulcerative colitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties. The study, which systematically reviewed 68 experimental studies on medicinal plants and ulcerative colitis, found that plants from various families, including Asteraceae and Lamiaceae, showed promising results in reducing inflammation in experimental animals. Specifically, plants such as Andrographis paniculata and Punica granatum, which are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, demonstrated potential in treating ulcerative colitis, suggesting that herbal capsules containing these plants could be beneficial for patients with the condition.
